challenges.explanation: break exits the loop immediately when n equals 30, so 30 itself is never printed. Only 10 and 20 are printed before the break fires.

challenges.prompt: This code is supposed to print only the positive numbers, but it prints all of them. What is wrong?
options: break should be continue — break exits the whole loop, but we only want to skip negative numbers
The condition should be n > 0 instead of n < 0
print(n) should come before the if statement
challenges.explanation: break stops the entire loop the moment it hits a negative number. Using continue instead tells Python to skip just that iteration and keep going, so all positive numbers are printed.

challenges.prompt: Complete the code so it prints every word except "skip".
challenges.explanation: continue tells Python to skip the rest of the loop body for this iteration and move to the next word, so "skip" is never passed to print.

challenges.prompt: Put these lines in the right order so the code searches for "mango" and prints a message if it is not found.
challenges.explanation: The list comes first, then the for loop. Inside the loop we check for the target and break if found. The else block is at the same indentation as for — it runs only when no break occurred, meaning the target was never found.
